codeReview.summary: The fix normalizes saved/deep-linked tag lines at the dashboard boundary, resets controlled inputs before running a saved lookup, and listens for hash changes so route hydration no longer reintroduces duplicated tag text. The diff and regression tests cover the malformed tag and saved-lookup replacement cases described in the feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skipped the web-feature-worker browser-verification step and relied on automated tests only for this user-facing dashboard change. The skill may need a documented exception for fully deterministic route/state regressions, or the feature handoff should capture stronger evidence when browser verification is omitted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false with deviation on step 6; transcript skeleton for worker session 852c1873-e6ca-426f-b713-e3511a25cd4e contains no agent-browser usage even though the skill marks it required whenever available.

summary: Reviewed commit 674ecc5 and the worker transcript/handoff for fix-saved-lookup-tagline-reset. I found no blocking or non-blocking correctness issues; the implementation matches the feature requirements and regression coverage is in place, with one shared-state observation about the skill/browser-validation procedure.
